opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I smashed my leg into the edge of my heavy oak table a few hours ago, and it swelled straight up into a hard, painful lump. It's really sore when I move it — should I be worried?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The swelling came up so fast and the lump feels hard, so I wanted to check I haven't done something serious to my leg.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 3 hours ago, right after I walked hard into the corner of a solid wooden table
symptom_narrative.character: a deep ache, and there's a firm swollen lump where I hit it
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: pretty sore, worse when I move my leg or press on the spot
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the front of my right thigh, where it hit the table
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it happened, worse with movement
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: keeping still, and the ice pack took the edge off
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving my leg, bending my knee, or pressing on it
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no numbness or pins and needles that I've noticed, and the skin isn't cut or broken
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no, first time I've done anything like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm walking with a limp and it's hard to bend my knee all the way

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone and is a bit worried about managing on his own with a sore leg

objective_data_offered.value: a firm swollen lump about the size of my palm on the front of my right thigh where I hit it
an early red-purple bruise coming up over the sore spot; the skin isn't broken or cut
I can still stand and walk on it, just with a limp because it hurts
I put a frozen gel pack on it for about 20 minutes before I called
